title: Remove mute option — music plays on by default with no pause/mute capability

requirements.summary: Remove all pause/mute logic from AppContext.tsx and make background music autoplay on loop with silent error handling
acceptanceCriteria: Background music begins playing automatically when the app loads without any user interaction.
There is no isMusicPlaying state or toggleMusic function in AppContext.tsx.
Audio is set to loop = true and volume = 1.0.
If autoplay is blocked, a one-time document interaction listener retries audio.play() silently.
All audio operations are wrapped in try/catch that fail silently — the app never crash.

file_operations.description: Remove the isMusicPlaying state and toggleMusic handler entirely. Modify the audio initialization to attempt autoplay immediately on mount with loop = true and volume = 1.0. Add a one-time document interaction listener (click/keydown) that retries audio.play() silently if autoplay is blocked. Wrap all audio operations in try/catch blocks that fail silently to ensure the app never crashes regardless of audio file availability or playback restrictions.

requirements.summary: Remove the music toggle button from AppShell.tsx UI
acceptanceCriteria: The music toggle button is no longer rendered anywhere in AppShell.tsx.
No references to isMusicPlaying or toggleMusic remain in AppShell.tsx.
The dark mode toggle and all other AppShell UI elements remain unchanged.
No TypeScript errors result from removing the music button references.

file_operations.description: Remove the music toggle button JSX and all references to isMusicPlaying and toggleMusic from the AppContext. Preserve the dark mode toggle button and all other UI elements including ambient glow overlays and floating hearts background. Ensure no TypeScript errors remain after removing the music control references.
